title: Getting started
text: new_var_data() takes three required inputs (empirical data, model predictions, residuals), T × p numeric matrix (time points × variables). Simulated data posterior predictive checks optional enables rightmost column diagnostic grid. example simulates simple two-variable VAR(1) process directly, without modelling package.
code: # Simulate a VAR(1) process T  <- 150 p  <- 4 phi <- matrix(c(0.5, 0.1, 0.0, 0.0,                 0.2, 0.4, 0.0, 0.1,                 0.0, 0.0, 0.6, 0.0,                 0.1, 0.0, 0.2, 0.3), nrow = p, byrow = TRUE)  emp <- matrix(0, T, p) for (t in 2:T) {   emp[t, ] <- phi %*% emp[t - 1, ] + rnorm(p, sd = 0.5) }  # Fit a simple lag-1 linear model per variable (stand-in for a real VAR fit) pred <- matrix(NA, T, p) res  <- matrix(NA, T, p) for (j in seq_len(p)) {   fit        <- lm(emp[-1, j] ~ emp[-T, j])   pred[-1, j] <- predict(fit)   res[-1, j]  <- residuals(fit) }  # Simulate from the fitted model for the posterior predictive check sim <- matrix(0, T, p) for (t in 2:T) {   sim[t, ] <- phi %*% sim[t - 1, ] + rnorm(p, sd = 0.5) } vd <- new_var_data(   empirical  = emp,   predicted  = pred,   residuals  = res,   simulated  = sim,   var_names  = c("Mood", "Energy", "Fatigue", "Anxiety") )  vd #> <var_data> #>   Subjects  : 1  #>   Variables : 4 ( Mood, Energy, Fatigue, Anxiety ) #>   Timepoints  : 150  #>   Components: empirical, predicted, residuals, simulated

title: Getting started
text: plot_var_check() returns patchwork object, can printed, saved ggplot2::ggsave(), combined plots.  row shows one variable. columns : Empirical & Predicted: observed values (black) predictions (orange), R² RMSE bottom right. small panel right marginal histogram Gaussian overlay. Residuals: residuals time, annotated AR(1) coefficient 95% CI flag autocorrelation. Residuals vs. Predicted: scatter residuals predictions. clear pattern signals model misspecification. Simulated: data simulated fitted model, visual comparison empirical series.

title: Getting started
text: Show subset variables name index:  Drop columns need. layout adjusts automatically:
code: plot_var_check(vd, vars = c("Mood", "Fatigue")) plot_var_check(vd, panels = c("data", "residuals"))

title: Getting started
text: data comes multiple people, pass list matrices (one per subject) new_var_data(). Use subject argument choose one plot.
code: emp2  <- emp + matrix(rnorm(T * p, sd = 0.2), T, p) pred2 <- pred + matrix(rnorm(T * p, sd = 0.1), T, p) res2  <- emp2 - pred2  vd_multi <- new_var_data(   empirical = list(emp, emp2),   predicted = list(pred, pred2),   residuals = list(res, res2),   simulated = list(sim, sim),   var_names = c("Mood", "Energy", "Fatigue", "Anxiety") )  plot_var_check(vd_multi, subject = 2)

title: Getting started
text: Colours: pass named list empirical /predicted keys. Unspecified keys retain defaults.  Theme: default theme_varcheck(). Add ggplot2::theme() call top via theme argument.  Shared y-limits: default, limits computed across selected variables. Override comparing across subjects model variants.
code: plot_var_check(vd, colors = list(predicted = "steelblue4")) plot_var_check(   vd,   theme = ggplot2::theme(     text      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9, family = "sans"),     panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()   ) ) plot_var_check(vd, ylim_data = c(-3, 3), ylim_res = c(-2, 2))

title: Getting started
text: fit model mlVAR, residuals() predict() methods return per-subject data frames. code shows assemble var_data object. new_var_data() requires numeric matrices, call .matrix() data frame. Assume already run: Single subject subjects Pass list matrices (one per subject) plot individual later subject = .
code: library(mlVAR)  mlVAR_out <- mlVAR(data, vars = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),                    idvar = "id", lags = 1,                    dayvar = "day", beepvar = "beep")  pred_df <- predict(mlVAR_out) res_df  <- residuals(mlVAR_out) sim_out <- mlVAR:::resimulate(mlVAR_out, keep_missing = TRUE,                               variance = "empirical") i <- 1  # subject index  vd <- new_var_data(   empirical = as.matrix(data[data$id == u_ptp[i], vars]),   predicted = as.matrix(pred_df[pred_df$id == u_ptp[i], vars]),   residuals = as.matrix(res_df[res_df$id == u_ptp[i], vars]),   simulated = as.matrix(sim_out[sim_out$id == u_ptp[i], vars]) )  plot_var_check(vd) vars     <- c("A", "B", "C", "D") n_subj   <- length(u_ptp)  vd_all <- new_var_data(   empirical = lapply(u_ptp, \(id) as.matrix(data[data$id == id, vars])),   predicted = lapply(u_ptp, \(id) as.matrix(pred_df[pred_df$id == id, vars])),   residuals = lapply(u_ptp, \(id) as.matrix(res_df[res_df$id == id, vars])),   simulated = lapply(u_ptp, \(id) as.matrix(sim_out[sim_out$id == id, vars])) )  plot_var_check(vd_all, subject = 3)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Björn S. Siepe. Author, maintainer, copyright holder. Jonas M. B. Haslbeck. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Haslbeck J, Jongerling J, Siepe B, Epskamp S, Waldorp L (2026). “Model Checking Vector Autoregressive Models.” PsyArXiv. doi:10.31234/osf.io/k6uz4.
code: @Article{,   title = {Model Checking for Vector Autoregressive Models},   author = {Jonas M. B. Haslbeck and Joran Jongerling and Björn S. Siepe and Sacha Epskamp and Lourens Waldorp},   journal = {PsyArXiv},   year = {2026},   doi = {10.31234/osf.io/k6uz4}, }

title: VARcheck
text: VARcheck produces diagnostic grids vector autoregressive (VAR) models. Given model’s empirical data, predictions, residuals, assembles multi-panel figure makes quality fit visible. package model-agnostic: works VAR implementation (mlVAR, vars, DSEM, custom code) long can supply matrix empirical values, matrix predictions, matrix residuals. information use package, see extended documentation package website.

title: VARcheck
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub:

title: VARcheck
text: Wrap data var_data object, call plot_var_check(). provide longer instruction package vignette, basic workflow looks like : row output corresponds one variable contains four column groups: Marginal histograms Gaussian overlay appear next time-series panel.
code: library(VARcheck)  vd <- new_var_data(   empirical  = emp,     # T × p matrix of observed values   predicted  = pred,    # T × p matrix of model predictions   residuals  = res,     # T × p matrix of residuals   simulated  = sim,     # T × p matrix of posterior-predictive simulations (optional)   var_names  = c("Mood", "Energy", "Fatigue", "Anxiety") )  plot_var_check(vd)

title: VARcheck
text: Select variables panels Multiple subjects Pass list matrices (one per subject) new_var_data(). Use subject argument select one plot. Colours theme default theme theme_varcheck(), minimal ggplot2 theme can use .
code: # Show only two variables plot_var_check(vd, vars = c("Mood", "Energy"))  # Drop the simulated column plot_var_check(vd, panels = c("data", "residuals", "scatter")) vd_multi <- new_var_data(   empirical = list(emp_s1, emp_s2, emp_s3),   predicted = list(pred_s1, pred_s2, pred_s3),   residuals = list(res_s1, res_s2, res_s3) )  plot_var_check(vd_multi, subject = 2) # Change line colours (partial override merges with defaults) plot_var_check(vd, colors = list(predicted = "steelblue"))  # Override any theme element plot_var_check(vd, theme = ggplot2::theme(text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9)))

title: VARcheck
text: Please cite following paper using package: Haslbeck, J. M. B. et al. (2026). Model Checking Vector Autoregressive Models https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/k6uz4

what: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
title: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
text: Constructs `var_data` object used input [plot_var_check()]. component either single `T x p` numeric matrix (single subject) list `T_i x p` matrices (multiple subjects). Single matrices coerced length-1 list automatically.

title: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
code: new_var_data(   empirical,   predicted,   residuals,   simulated = NULL,   var_names = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'var_data' print(x, ...)

title: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
text: empirical Numeric matrix list matrices. Observed values. predicted Numeric matrix list matrices. Model predictions. residuals Numeric matrix list matrices. Residuals (`empirical - predicted`). simulated Optional numeric matrix list matrices. Data simulated fitted model. Required plotting `"simulated"` panel. var_names Optional character vector length `p` variable names used plot labels. Defaults `"V1"`, `"V2"`, etc. x `var_data` object. ... used; present S3 method compatibility.

title: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
text: object class `var_data`.

title: Create a VAR data object — new_var_data
code: set.seed(1) emp <- matrix(rnorm(75), nrow = 100, ncol = 2) #> Warning: data length [75] is not a sub-multiple or multiple of the number of rows [100] pred <- emp + matrix(rnorm(75, sd = 0.3), 100, 2) #> Warning: data length [75] is not a sub-multiple or multiple of the number of rows [100] res <- emp - pred sim <- matrix(rnorm(75), 100, 2) #> Warning: data length [75] is not a sub-multiple or multiple of the number of rows [100] vd <- new_var_data(emp, pred, res, sim, var_names = c("Mood", "Energy"))

title: Plot VAR model diagnostics — plot_var_check
text: Creates multi-panel diagnostic grid fitted VAR model. row corresponds one variable; columns show () empirical data vs. predictions, (b) residuals time, (c) residuals vs. predictions scatter, (d) data simulated estimated model. time-series panel accompanied marginal histogram Gaussian overlay.

title: Plot VAR model diagnostics — plot_var_check
code: plot_var_check(   data,   subject = 1,   vars = NULL,   panels = c("data", "residuals", "scatter", "simulated"),   colors = list(),   theme = NULL,   ylim_data = NULL,   ylim_res = NULL )

title: Plot VAR model diagnostics — plot_var_check
text: data `var_data` object created [new_var_data()]. subject Integer. Index subject plot. Defaults `1`. vars Character integer vector selecting variables include. Defaults variables. panels Character vector controlling columns shown. subset `c("data", "residuals", "scatter", "simulated")`, order. Defaults four. colors Named list controlling line colours. Recognised elements: `empirical` (default `"black"`) `predicted` (default `"darkorange2"`). Partial lists merged defaults. theme `ggplot2::theme()` object added top [theme_varcheck()]. Use override individual theme elements. ylim_data Numeric vector length 2. Shared y-limits data-scale panels (empirical/predicted/simulated). Auto-computed data `NULL`. ylim_res Numeric vector length 2. Shared y-limits residual panels. Auto-computed residuals `NULL`.

title: Plot VAR model diagnostics — plot_var_check
text: `patchwork` object.

title: Plot VAR model diagnostics — plot_var_check
code: set.seed(1) emp <- matrix(rnorm(300), nrow = 100, ncol = 3) pred <- emp + matrix(rnorm(300, sd = 0.3), 100, 3) res <- emp - pred sim <- matrix(rnorm(300), 100, 3) vd <- new_var_data(emp, pred, res, sim, var_names = c("X1", "X2", "X3")) # \donttest{ plot_var_check(vd)  # }

title: Default VARcheck ggplot2 theme — theme_varcheck
text: minimal theme used default style VARcheck plots. Pass `ggplot2::theme()` object `theme` argument [plot_var_check()] override individual elements top base.

title: Default VARcheck ggplot2 theme — theme_varcheck
text: `ggplot2` theme object.

title: VARcheck 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. new_var_data(): constructs model-agnostic var_data object empirical data, predictions, residuals, optional simulated data. Accepts single matrix (single subject) list matrices (multiple subjects). plot_var_check(): assembles multi-panel diagnostic grid — one row per variable — empirical vs. predicted time series, residual inspection, residuals vs. predictions scatter, optional posterior predictive check panels. theme_varcheck(): default minimal ggplot2 theme used plot_var_check(), available standalone use.
